task_completion.description: Open the OpenRocket application and load the rocket designs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket GUI is visible with one or more of the specified .ork files (simple_model_rocket.ork, dual_parachute_deployment.ork, clustered_motors.ork) loaded.

task_completion.description: Run flight simulations to obtain the required data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket simulation window is open, showing completed simulations with flight data such as max altitude, velocity, ground hit velocity, and stability.

task_completion.description: Compile the flight data into fleet_summary.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or spreadsheet application displays fleet_summary.csv containing columns for max altitude, max velocity, ground hit velocity, and stability margin for all three rockets.
A text editor displays launch_day_briefing.txt being edited, listing the three rockets.

task_completion.description: Flag the dual parachute rocket as UNSAFE and NO-GO.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The launch_day_briefing.txt file explicitly marks 'dual_parachute_deployment.ork' as UNSAFE and provides a NO-GO recommendation due to high ground hit velocity (> 9 m/s).

task_completion.description: Provide GO recommendations for the safe rockets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The launch_day_briefing.txt file provides a GO recommendation for the other rockets (simple_model_rocket.ork and clustered_motors.ork) assuming they meet the safety thresholds.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to run the simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenRocket application being actively used to run simulations, rather than relying on external scripts or guessing.

integrity.description: The data entered into the CSV and briefing matches the simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values for altitude, velocity, ground hit velocity, and stability in the CSV/TXT files correspond to the numbers visible in the OpenRocket simulation results window.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a text editor or spreadsheet to create the export files.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, mousepad) or spreadsheet application is visible on screen with the contents of the CSV or TXT files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires evaluating three OpenRocket designs: simple_model_rocket.ork, dual_parachute_deployment.ork, and clustered_motors.ork. The simulations for these files are initially outdated and must be run in OpenRocket. The 'dual_parachute_deployment.ork' file has been intentionally mutated to have a critically small main parachute, resulting in an excessive ground hit velocity (> 9 m/s). The agent must detect this and explicitly flag it as UNSAFE and NO-GO in the launch_day_briefing.txt file. The other rockets should be evaluated based on the > 9 m/s ground hit velocity and < 1.0 stability margin thresholds.
